title: RIOT Rockdale Facility Strategic Evolution and Mining Operations
text: The Rockdale facility, acquired through RIOT's $651 million acquisition of Whinstone US in 2021, has been critical to the company's mining capacity expansion. The facility provides 700 megawatts of existing power infrastructure, making it one of RIOT's largest operational sites. In Q2 2024, the company acquired Block Mining Inc., which had access to 60 megawatts of operational capacity at Rockdale, with potential to expand to over 300 megawatts. During 2024, the company also acquired mining operations from Rhodium that were hosted at Rockdale, gaining access to an additional 125 megawatts of previously contracted power capacity. In January 2026, the company entered into the long-term data center lease agreement with AMD at Rockdale, initially for 25 megawatts of critical IT load capacity, with the first 5 megawatts delivered on schedule in January 2026 and the remaining 20 megawatts anticipated for delivery in May 2026. The Rockdale facility is expected to eventually host up to 200 megawatts of expanded AMD capacity under extension options. The facility has also been the focus of engineering improvements, with the company utilizing its ESS Metron engineering division to optimize electrical infrastructure design and reduce capital expenditure requirements.
